evals.prompt: I have a customer asking about slow Odoo performance, not sure where to start
evals.expected_output: Intake asks clarifying question (performance is not directly in routing table; should escalate or route to odoo-deprecation-audit / odoo-customization-inventory based on user's follow-up)

evals.prompt: I want to check if Acme's Odoo system has any issues
evals.expected_output: Intake outputs collision-resolution template offering odoo-risk-overview vs odoo-customization-inventory (Example 3 case)

evals.prompt: Customer says Odoo is missing feature X — help me respond
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-objection-handling with Collision 1 reasoning

evals.prompt: I don't know what's new in Odoo 18 — write slides for a meeting
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-feature-highlights with Collision 2 reasoning (slide = marketing output)

evals.prompt: Someone asked me if Odoo can handle multi-level approvals
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-feature-check (single-feature availability) or odoo-capability-proof if proof-package signal; row 6 vs row 10

evals.prompt: Please review this patch for me
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-code-review (row 13: review EXISTING code)

evals.prompt: Customer requested 20 features — which does Odoo have, which need building?
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-gap-analysis (row 7: multi-requirement effort matrix)

evals.prompt: I have a long Odoo code snippet, not sure how to review it
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-code-review (row 13: review existing code)

evals.prompt: This customer's loyalty module, break it down for me
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-customization-inventory OR asks clarifying question (could be feature-check if 'loyalty' module is known); ambiguity expected

evals.prompt: Help me audit the system before upgrading
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-deprecation-audit (row 4: audit before upgrade) OR odoo-risk-overview if exec framing

evals.prompt: Write a computed field calculating total_amount for sale.order
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — direct match to odoo-backend-coding (row 12: write code)

evals.prompt: Customer asks about API differences between v16 and v17
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — direct match to odoo-version-diff (row 5: explicit API diff)

evals.prompt: Review this Python code (paste below)
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — direct match to odoo-code-review

evals.prompt: what's the weather in Hanoi
evals.expected_output: Intake declines politely (non-Odoo intent)

evals.prompt: /odoo-semantic-mcp:connect
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — explicit slash command

evals.prompt: Review the sale order form in my Odoo 17 browser before the demo — does it look right and is it accessible?
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-ui-review (row 28: rate a RENDERED screen in the browser, vs odoo-code-review which reads source statically)

evals.prompt: OWL component không hiện, trang trắng và có console error — tìm nguyên nhân giúp tôi
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-debug (row 29: find ROOT CAUSE of a broken screen at runtime, vs odoo-ui-review which rates a working screen)

evals.prompt: Compare the before/after screenshots of the kanban view to see if my CSS change caused any visual drift
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-visual-regression (row 30: diff TWO states/builds for drift, vs odoo-ui-review which judges ONE screen once)

evals.prompt: Record a short demo video of the inventory app walkthrough on my live instance
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends odoo-demo-recording (row 31: produce a REAL video/GIF of a live instance, vs odoo-content-draft which writes the script only)

evals.prompt: First time setting up the visual stack — wire the browser MCP and configure my instance URL
evals.expected_output: Intake recommends /odoo-semantic-skills:setup command (row 32: one-time environment bootstrap for the visual stack, vs odoo-onboarding which bootstraps project CODE context)

evals.prompt: I'm not sure what I need but I have a big Odoo project starting next month
evals.expected_output: Intake fires (open-ended, no concrete verb). Enters brainstorm: reads .odoo-ai/context.md, presents 3 clarifying options (engineering setup, sales/pre-sales, strategic planning). Proposes Proposed Plan with domain + approach + gate. Does NOT write any files this turn.

evals.prompt: help me think through the upgrade from v16 to v17
evals.expected_output: Intake fires (vague multi-step). Tier 3 may match row 27 (upgrade plan). Presents fast-path gate: 'Plan: run /odoo-plan-upgrade. Proceed?' OR enters brainstorm if upgrade scope is unclear. Does NOT write files.

evals.prompt: I need to win this deal with Acme — help me figure out what to do
evals.expected_output: Intake fires (business-outcome-first, no concrete verb). Enters brainstorm: clarifying options (pre-sales gap analysis, objection handling, full bid response). Proposes plan with skill chain. Iron Law: no execution before user approves.

evals.prompt: where do I begin with this Odoo project
evals.expected_output: Intake fires (explicit 'where do I begin' trigger phrase from description). Enters brainstorm or suggests odoo-onboarding if .odoo-ai/context.md is absent. Presents Proposed Plan gate.

evals.prompt: I have 1200 requirements to classify and scope for a prospect
evals.expected_output: Intake fires (large BRL job, row 33). Tier 3 matches 'BRL' + large-scale signal. Fast-path gate: 'Plan: run odoo-brl to classify and cost 1200 requirements. Proceed?' Does NOT enter brainstorm — scale + classify signal is a direct Tier-3 hit to odoo-brl.

evals.prompt: diff v16 v17
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — intent is clear, single-step, matches exactly one specialist (odoo-version-diff row 5). Fast-path fires directly without intake brainstorm.

evals.prompt: write computed field _compute_amount_total on sale.order
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — intent is clear, single-step, explicit 'write computed field' = odoo-backend-coding Tier-1 exact match. Direct fire.

evals.prompt: review this PR #42
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — 'review this PR' is Tier-1 exact match to odoo-code-review. Direct fire.

evals.prompt: /odoo-respond-bid
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — explicit slash command. Harness invokes the command directly.

evals.prompt: /odoo-plan-upgrade
evals.expected_output: Intake does NOT fire — explicit slash command. Harness invokes the command directly.

evals.prompt: I need to win this deal but I already told the customer we'd do a gap analysis — now what
evals.expected_output: Intake fires (in-flight multi-step, next action unclear). Brainstorm: resume prior thread (check state.json), or offer options: continue with odoo-gap-analysis, or layer in odoo-objection-handling / odoo-discovery-summary. Full Proposed Plan gate.

evals.prompt: I need to classify 1200 requirements for this RFP and get a cost estimate with a dependency graph
evals.expected_output: Intake fires Tier-3 row 33. All three BRL signals present: scale (1200), cost estimate, dependency graph. Fast-path gate: 'Plan: run odoo-brl. Proceed?' MUST NOT route to odoo-gap-analysis (no cost/DAG there) or brainstorm. Fails correctly if routed to gap-analysis or brainstorm.

evals.prompt: Can you generate a full QA test suite for the sale order module including edge cases and a pre-deploy checklist?
evals.expected_output: Intake fires Tier-3 row 34. Signals: 'full QA suite', 'test cases', 'pre-deploy checklist'. Routes to qa-suite workflow via fast-path gate. MUST NOT route to odoo-code-review (static review) or odoo-deploy-checklist alone.

evals.prompt: We have a support ticket: customer says the invoice form is broken after upgrade — triage it and draft a response
evals.expected_output: Intake fires Tier-3 row 35. Signals: 'support ticket', 'triage', 'draft response'. Routes to support-triage workflow via fast-path gate. MUST NOT route to odoo-debug (dev session) or odoo-deal-followup (sales email).

evals.prompt: Help me debug this OWL widget — console error, page is white, need a sustained session to trace the root cause across multiple steps
evals.expected_output: Intake fires Tier-3 row 38. 'Sustained troubleshooting session' + 'multi-step' signals distinguish from odoo-debug (single-turn). Routes to ui-debug-session workflow. Fails correctly if routed to odoo-debug.

evals.prompt: I need to take a feature brief all the way to a published blog post — draft, internal review, then publish
evals.expected_output: Intake fires Tier-3 row 39. Signals: 'brief to publish', 'draft then review then publish'. Routes to content-production workflow. MUST NOT route to odoo-content-draft (single piece only) or odoo-campaign-plan (plans the campaign, not produces).

evals.prompt: Manage the full sales closing cycle for the Acme deal — follow-ups, objection handling, and final close
evals.expected_output: Intake fires Tier-3 row 37. Signals: 'full sales closing cycle', 'multi-step'. Routes to sales-closing-cycle workflow. MUST NOT route to odoo-deal-followup (single email) or /odoo-respond-bid (RFP document).

evals.prompt: Customer needs only 3 features: multi-currency, batch invoicing, approval. Which does Odoo have and what's the effort?
evals.expected_output: Intake Tier-3 Collision 8 resolution: short ad-hoc list (3 items), no cost/DAG signal. Routes to odoo-gap-analysis. MUST NOT route to odoo-brl (no scale or cost/RTM/DAG requirement). Fails correctly if routed to odoo-brl.

evals.prompt: I want to do something with Odoo for our sales team
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 GATE fires: intent is vague (no concrete verb, no domain, purpose unclear). Intake MUST NOT proceed to Phase R (Recon) or produce a Proposed Plan until all three GATE items are resolved: WHAT the user wants (intent), WHY (purpose), and WHAT done looks like (expected outcomes). Intake presents pre-structured options — e.g. '(a) automate follow-up emails, (b) configure a sales pipeline, (c) assess what Odoo can do for your team' — and waits for the user to select before continuing. Fails correctly if it skips the gate and jumps to Recon or a Proposed Plan.

evals.prompt: I need to add a custom approval step to the sale order workflow — can you survey what already exists in our codebase before planning?
evals.expected_output: Phase 0 gate closes immediately (intent = add approval step, purpose = extend sale order workflow, expected outcome = code implementing the approval gate). Phase R (Recon) then dispatches ≤1–2 READ-ONLY agents (e.g. Explore or odoo-override-finding in read-only mode) or OSM tools (find_override_point, model_inspect) to map existing override points and current workflow code. Phase R agents MUST NOT write any file and MUST NOT spawn further sub-agents. After Recon, intake writes the Proposed Plan enriched with Recon findings (hook location, relevant method names). Plan Mode is required because the Approach is odoo-backend-coding (writes-files). Fails correctly if Phase R writes a file or spawns a sub-agent.

evals.prompt: Add a `priority` field to sale.order (backend) and render it as a star widget on the form (frontend) — multi-file change, I want the full plan
evals.expected_output: Intake fires (full-stack request spanning backend + frontend). After Phase 0 gate + Phase R Recon, the Proposed Plan includes Plan Mode: required. Inside Plan Mode the implementation plan MUST contain all three blocks: (1) Workitem list — WI-A (odoo-backend-coding: adds Python field + XML attribute; files: models/sale_order.py, views/sale_order_form.xml) and WI-B (odoo-frontend-coding: adds OWL star widget; files: static/src/components/priority_widget.js, static/src/components/priority_widget.xml) — file sets are DISJOINT; (2) Dependency graph — linear topology, edge WI-B --data-flow--> WI-A (widget binds to field that must exist first), with topological_order [WI-A, WI-B] and critical_path [WI-A, WI-B]; (3) Assignment — WI-A: odoo-backend-coding (sonnet, M); WI-B: odoo-frontend-coding (sonnet, M) — each with acceptance criteria and verify command. Fails correctly if any of the three Plan Mode blocks is absent or if file sets overlap.

evals.prompt: [User has approved the Proposed Plan for a sale.order field change; Plan Mode UI is shown; user types: 'Actually the field should be on purchase.order, not sale.order — please revise']
evals.expected_output: Plan Mode rejection flow: user refines inside Plan Mode UI (step 5 of the procedure). Intake MUST loop back to the soft-plan-gate (NOT to execution). It updates the Proposed Plan with purchase.order as the target, adjusts Workitem list (files: models/purchase_order.py, views/purchase_order_form.xml) and Assignment accordingly, then re-emits the Proposed Plan gate for re-approval. MUST NOT dispatch a writes-files specialist off a rejected plan. Re-enters Plan Mode only after the revised plan is re-approved at the text gate. Fails correctly if it dispatches odoo-backend-coding before the revised Proposed Plan is re-approved.

evals.prompt: Does Odoo 17 CE support multi-currency invoicing out of the box?
evals.expected_output: Regression: Intake Tier-3 row 6 matches odoo-feature-check (single feature lookup, chat-only output_mode). Plan Mode decision tree: output_mode = chat-only → SKIP Plan Mode (unchanged behavior). Intake ends its turn with a fast-path gate ('Plan: run odoo-feature-check to verify multi-currency invoicing availability in Odoo 17 CE. Proceed?'). On approval, the specialist fires via the Agent tool on the next turn — NO EnterPlanMode call. Fails correctly if Plan Mode is entered for a chat-only skill.

notes: 43 eval cases total: IDs 1-20 ported from the prior router skill (updated skill_name to 'intake'); IDs 21-31 are new cases covering vague->brainstorm (21-24, 31), clear->fast-path (26-28), slash->no-fire (29-30), BRL-handoff (25 updated); IDs 32-38 cover new routing rows 33-39: brl-handoff (32), qa-suite (33), support-triage (34), ui-debug-session (35), content-production (36), sales-closing-cycle (37), BRL-vs-gap-analysis collision (38). IDs 39-43 cover the 5-phase planner behavior added by WI-A: phase-0-gate-mandatory (39), phase-r-recon-dispatch (40), plan-mode-full-schema with workitem+DAG+assignment (41), plan-mode-rejection-flow (42), plan-mode-regression-chat-only-skip (43). Full Mode 5 trigger optimization scheduled for Phase D per AC-D6 of the v1.0.0 refinement plan. Phase A acceptance is verified via inline walkthrough by main agent (see report).
